sector_rotation.explanation: No clear rotation signal. Cyclical and defensive sectors are performing similarly — market may be in a transitional phase.

news_headlines.title: Tesla earnings rise, but AI expenses add up for Elon Musk - Axios
news_headlines.description: Tesla reported an uptick in quarterly revenue and profit, but the costs of pivoting to an AI future are starting to add up.

news_headlines.title: Flurry Of Protests As WBD Shareholders Set To Vote On Paramount Merger - Deadline
news_headlines.description: A new video and a press conference slammed the pending Warner Bros. Discovery-Paramount merger with a rally planned in NYC Thursday.

news_headlines.title: Anthropic has surged to a trillion-dollar valuation on secondary markets, overtaking OpenAI. - Business Insider
news_headlines.description: Buyers are in a race to secure a dwindling supply of shares in Anthropic, driving the AI company's valuation on some platforms to $1 trillion.

news_headlines.title: United Airlines CEO makes stark warning about ticket prices - thestreet.com
news_headlines.description: Scott Kirby warned that ticket prices could soon rise by up to 20%.

news_headlines.title: Mark Zuckerberg sends shocking message to Meta employees - thestreet.com
news_headlines.description: Meta just crossed a line most companies have avoided

news_headlines.title: StanChart $95 Per Barrel Is The New Oil Price Equilibrium - Crude Oil Prices Today | OilPrice.com
news_headlines.description: Analysts expect sustained higher oil prices, even post-conflict, while gas markets remain relatively stable due to ample supply.

news_headlines.title: Former congressman Devin Nunes departs as CEO of Trump media company - The Washington Post
news_headlines.description: The onetime lawmaker is leaving Trump Media & Technology, which operates the social media platform Truth Social.

news_headlines.title: Tesla will build factories just to retrofit millions of HW3 cars it said could do FSD - Electrek
news_headlines.description: Tesla said every car had the hardware to drive itself. That was false, so it will build factories in every city to retrofit millions of cars.

news_headlines.title: Airline company Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights as war squeezes fuel prices and supplies - AP News
news_headlines.description: The German company that owns Lufthansa Airlines and other European carriers says it plans to cut 20,000 short-haul flights through October as the Iran war drives up oil prices and deepens worries that some countries may run low on jet fuel. The Lufthansa Grou…

news_headlines.title: S&P futures are little changed following another record-setting day for benchmark: Live updates - CNBC
news_headlines.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite rallied to new closing records on Wednesday afternoon.

news_headlines.title: Andreessen, Thrive Poised for Windfall From SpaceX’s Cursor Bid - Bloomberg.com
news_headlines.description: Andreessen Horowitz and Thrive Capital are poised for a multibillion-dollar windfall from their early investments in Cursor if Elon Musk’s SpaceX moves forward with an agreement to acquire the artificial intelligence coding startup for $60 billion. Andreessen…

news_headlines.title: John Ternus’s net worth as Apple’s next CEO - thestreet.com
news_headlines.description: John Ternus will take over as Apple’s CEO in September 2026 when Tim Cook steps down Here’s how much Ternus is worth and how much money he’s likely to make.

news_headlines.title: Trump team backs away from gasoline price promises - Politico
news_headlines.description: Administration officials on Wednesday softened their predictions of an end to the Iran war and its turbulent effects on energy prices.

news_headlines.title: ServiceNow Earnings Meet Views As Armis Deal Clouds Margin Outlook - Investor's Business Daily
news_headlines.description: Enterprise software maker ServiceNow reported Q1 earnings that met estimates while revenue edged by views. ServiceNow stock fell on the news.

news_headlines.title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Nasdaq Score Record Highs On War Ceasefire; Tesla Shares Jump (Live Coverage) - Investor's Business Daily
news_headlines.description: Stock Market Today: The Dow Jones index rises after President Donald Trump extends the U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Tesla earnings are due late.

nyt_headlines.title: A.I. Is Forcing More Belt-Tightening at Big Tech
nyt_headlines.abstract: The growing expense of keeping up in the artificial intelligence race is driving technology companies, young and old, to costs.

nyt_headlines.title: A.I. Start-Ups From Canada and Germany Merge to Take On Silicon Valley
nyt_headlines.abstract: Cohere is acquiring Aleph Alpha in a deal aimed at customers uneasy about the dominance of American companies in artificial intelligence.

nyt_headlines.title: Companies, Not Consumers, to Cash In Big From Tariff Refunds
nyt_headlines.abstract: Many families felt the sting of the president’s now-illegal tariffs, but companies have said little about whether they will share the $166 billion coming back to them.

nyt_headlines.title: Sam Altman’s Next High-Wire Act: Getting OpenAI to Make More Money
nyt_headlines.abstract: Mr. Altman, who has faced criticism over OpenAI’s direction, has culled company projects and is trying to be more disciplined with strategy.

nyt_headlines.title: You Don’t Have to Be Filthy Rich to Enjoy an Airport Shower
nyt_headlines.abstract: Affordable arrivals lounges offer amenities like nap pods and showers where weary passengers can freshen up. You may never start a vacation the same way again.

nyt_headlines.title: How Elon Musk Used SpaceX to Benefit Himself and His Businesses
nyt_headlines.abstract: The rocket maker has been a useful financial tool for Mr. Musk, providing the billionaire with loans and aiding his struggling companies, a Times examination found.

nyt_headlines.title: Who’s Who in ‘Michael’: A Guide to the Characters
nyt_headlines.abstract: A new Michael Jackson biopic, with the star’s nephew Jaafar Jackson in the title role, is packed with famous musical figures. These are some of them.

nyt_headlines.title: DeepSeek’s Sequel Set to Extend China’s Reach in Open-Source A.I.
nyt_headlines.abstract: Chinese companies have embraced making their most advanced artificial intelligence models available to all.

nyt_headlines.title: Who’s Getting a Tariff Refund?
nyt_headlines.abstract: Following a Supreme Court ruling that struck down several Trump administration tariffs, importers have begun applying for their share of $166 billion in refunds. As our economic policy reporter Tony Romm explains, consumers are unlikely to see much of that money returned to their own pockets.

nyt_headlines.title: War and Sanctions Accelerate China’s Currency Push
nyt_headlines.abstract: China’s bid to build a renminbi-based financial system beyond the U.S. dollar’s reach is gaining traction as a way to sidestep sanctions.

nyt_headlines.title: Pentagon Fires Stars and Stripes’ Advocate for Independence
nyt_headlines.abstract: The newspaper’s ombudsman, Jacqueline Smith, said she had been given no reason for her dismissal.

nyt_headlines.title: Nike to Cut 1,400 Jobs as Part of Its Turnaround Plan
nyt_headlines.abstract: Most of the layoffs will affect tech workers as the athletic giant tries to reverse a yearslong sales slump. It’s the second round of cuts this year.

nyt_headlines.title: Trump’s Fed Pick Faces Tough Task Shedding ‘Sock Puppet’ Label
nyt_headlines.abstract: Kevin M. Warsh, at his confirmation hearing to lead the Federal Reserve, sought to dispel concerns that he would cut interest rates to please President Trump.

nyt_headlines.title: Bessent Backs Financial Support for Oil-Rich U.A.E.
nyt_headlines.abstract: The Treasury secretary said that currency swap line would benefit both the United Arab Emirates and the United States.

nyt_headlines.title: U.S. Withholds Iraq’s Own Money Over Ties to Iran
nyt_headlines.abstract: The United States has halted dollar shipments to Iraq’s cash-based economy in an effort to force the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran. The money comes from Iraq’s oil revenues.
